subject: Physics
title: Kepler's Laws of Orbits
description: One-minute skill: Kepler's Laws of Orbits.

hook.text: Johannes Kepler discovered planets move in ellipses, not circles. It shocked the scientific world.

buildup.text: For centuries, astronomers forced planetary data into circular models. The math never quite fit.

discovery.text: Kepler found three laws: elliptical orbits, equal areas in equal times, and period links to distance.

twist.text: He had no idea why orbits were elliptical. It took Newton's gravity to explain the pattern.

climax.text: NASA still uses Kepler's laws to plan every spacecraft trajectory. They're that precise.

punchline.text: He mapped the orbits. Newton explained why.

quiz.question: What shape did Kepler discover planetary orbits actually are?
